description: The standard library's threading module. Rust threads are OS-level threads (1:1 threading model). Each thread has its own stack and runs in parallel. Data shared between threads must be Send and Sync. Python equivalent: threading.Thread (but without the GIL, so true parallelism).

methods.name: spawn
methods.signature: fn spawn<F, T>(f: F) -> JoinHandle<T> where F: FnOnce() -> T + Send + 'static, T: Send + 'static
methods.description: Spawns a new OS thread and returns a JoinHandle to wait for it. The closure must own all captured data (use move). Python equivalent: threading.Thread(target=fn).start().

methods.name: sleep
methods.description: Puts the current thread to sleep for the specified duration. Python equivalent: time.sleep().

methods.name: current
methods.description: Returns a handle to the current thread. You can use this to get the thread's name or ID.

methods.name: park
methods.description: Blocks the current thread until it receives an unpark() signal from another thread. Useful for building custom synchronization.

methods.name: Builder::new
methods.description: Creates a new thread builder for configuring a thread (name, stack size) before spawning it.

methods.name: JoinHandle::join
methods.description: Waits for the thread to finish and returns its result. Returns Err if the thread panicked. Python equivalent: thread.join().
methods.example:
let handle = thread::spawn(|| 42);
let result = handle.join().unwrap();
